_meta.title: CAG Audit Findings — Tamil Nadu
sources: CAG Reports on State Finances of Tamil Nadu (Annual)
CAG Reports on Revenue Receipts of Tamil Nadu
CAG Reports on PSUs of Tamil Nadu
Performance Audit Reports on various schemes
_meta.note: CAG = Comptroller and Auditor General of India. These are independent constitutional audit findings.

budget_deviations.note: Excess expenditure without appropriation = spending beyond what Parliament/Assembly approved — a constitutional violation requiring regularisation.

off_budget_borrowings.description: Borrowings made outside the state budget through SPVs, PSUs, and other entities — not reflected in official debt figures but ultimately the state's liability.
off_budget_borrowings.note: TVK White Paper 2026 confirmed off-budget borrowings significantly understate actual state obligations.

contingent_liabilities.description: Potential future payments — guarantees given by state to PSUs and other entities. If the entity defaults, state must pay.
contingent_liabilities.note: Many guarantees extended without following FRBM ceiling norms. Guarantee fee (meant to compensate state for risk) not regularly collected.

revenue_shortfalls.note: Tax arrears represent taxes assessed but not collected. Recovery rate has been declining, suggesting enforcement weakness.

key_findings.finding: CAG flagged ₹1.49 lakh crore in borrowings routed through SPVs and PSUs that were not reported in the state's fiscal deficit calculation.
key_findings.impact: Actual fiscal deficit was understated by approximately 2.1% of GSDP
key_findings.source: CAG Report on State Finances of TN, 2023-24

key_findings.finding: Electricity subsidy actually paid to TANGEDCO was ₹3,840 crore less than the subsidy amount shown in budget provisions, with the gap funded through TANGEDCO's own borrowings.
key_findings.impact: Masks true subsidy cost; pushes TANGEDCO further into debt
key_findings.source: CAG Report on PSUs of Tamil Nadu, 2022-23

key_findings.finding: Outstanding tax and non-tax revenue arrears reached ₹48,280 crore, with commercial tax arrears alone at ₹22,480 crore.
key_findings.impact: Significant revenue foregone; enforcement system needs reform
key_findings.source: CAG Report on Revenue Receipts of Tamil Nadu, 2022-23

key_findings.finding: Expenditure of ₹2,924 crore was incurred without legislative approval (Demands for Grants), requiring post-facto regularisation.
key_findings.impact: Violates Article 266 of Constitution; weakens legislative financial oversight
key_findings.source: CAG Report on State Finances of TN, 2021-22

key_findings.finding: State gave guarantees worth ₹1.27 lakh crore to PSUs but did not collect guarantee fees of ₹1,968 crore, violating FRBM provisions.
key_findings.impact: Revenue loss; implicit subsidy to inefficient PSUs
key_findings.source: CAG Report on State Finances of TN, 2021-22

key_findings.finding: Utilisation certificates for ₹3,840 crore of COVID relief funds were not submitted. Funds meant for migrant workers and health infrastructure had unverifiable end-use.
TNSTC accumulated losses reached ₹14,628 crore. Fare revision not done for over a decade. Maintenance backlog caused by inadequate funding.
key_findings.impact: Unsafe fleet; growing liability for state
key_findings.source: CAG Report on PSUs of Tamil Nadu, 2019-20
